name: Ray Evans
abstract: Raymond Bernard Evans (February 4, 1915 – February 15, 2007) was an American songwriter best known for being a half of a composing-songwriting duo with Jay Livingston, specializing himself in writing lyrics for film songs. On music Livingston composed, Evans wrote the lyrics.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: People had told me that before like…Jay Livingston and Ray Evans had a song in a C picture... for which they won the Academy Award
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: you did that with Jay Livingston and Ray Evans, guys who were born six weeks apart in 1950.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012
